title: socket.io Server with cors.origin: "*" — any website can open an authenticated socket to the API

params.passDetail: The socket.io Server pins cors.origin to an explicit allow-list (not "*") — only trusted front-ends can open a socket.
params.failDetail: new socket.io Server is created with cors: { origin: "*" }, allowing ANY website to open a Socket.IO connection to this server. Because the handshake carries the user's cookies, a malicious page can establish an authenticated real-time channel and drive or read events as the logged-in user. Set cors.origin to an explicit allow-list, not "*".
params.absentCallDetail: No socket.io Server construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The socket.io Server does not set cors.origin; verify the resolved origin is a trusted allow-list, not "*".

provenance.note: cors: { origin: "*" } is pasted to stop the browser blocking the socket during development. It ships and lets any origin ride the user's authenticated real-time channel.
